subject: uplift oparent version 1.2.1->1.2.3
commit_message:
uplift oparent version 1.2.1->1.2.3

Find one non-backward compatible change:
spring-web 5.0.9.RELEASE -> 5.1.2.RELEASE
String org.springframework.http.HttpStatus.toString()
return Integer.toString(this.value)//spring-web 5.0.9.RELEASE
return Integer.toString(this.value) + " " + name();//spring-web
5.1.2.RELEASE

Issue-ID: POLICY-1261
Change-Id: I23f7fe8dbd2d8b241c86ce910898a8df3641dbe5
Signed-off-by: Hengye <yehui.wang@est.tech>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

uplift oparent version 1.2.1->1.2.3

The uplift cause one non-backward change in policy/engine by:

spring-web 5.0.9.RELEASE -> 5.1.2.RELEASE
String org.springframework.http.HttpStatus.toString()
return Integer.toString(this.value)//spring-web 5.0.9.RELEASE
return Integer.toString(this.value) + " " + name();//spring-web 5.1.2.RELEASE

After this review is merged, I will push the fix of policy/engine that is caused by this NBC.

Patch Set 1:

The spring jar's are tricky - we inherit from Portal SDK some spring that they have not upgraded. Some of our policy/engine apps CAN upgrade spring components, but we have to make sure we do the right ones and make sure the portal ones don't clash. Otherwise the GUI doesn't come up.

Yehui - can you push a draft of the policy/engine review? I'd like to take a look at it as well as have Mike Mokry look at it. Its kind of tricky.

Patch Set 1:

Pam, I think we need to revert these changes, pap and pdp processes they come up and die.   I think it is because of these changes.  I am checking the lab timestamps and I am getting image created 2 hours ago, and the last policy/engine merge was one hour ago.   I would suspect that this also affects the CSIT tests.   I don't recall having this issue recently when I was working with these images.

Patch Set 1:

Or we can undo temporarily, I am finding issues here and there from moving to alpine, and I am not sure, what's introducing problems in kubernetes environment with the very latest.   Probably we should get all the alpine issues ironed out and then retry this one.

Patch Set 1:

The CSIT tests broke earlier than this merge. But I'm willing to revert it to see if that fixes it.

Patch Set 1:

Unfortunately it won't let me revert it. Not sure what is going on. Maybe its the INFO.yaml changes Jessica made??

Patch Set 1:

No worries Pam, thank you, trying to get to some conclusion, will try to revert to an older image in nexus3 and retry for comparison.   In the integration lab, it is using Alpine images (5 days old) and processes come up OK on the PAP side, but there are some other issues as I can see.

Patch Set 1:

Me too, this is bizarre. Maybe there is a compilation error somewhere? A merge failed??

Patch Set 1:

Tracking problem under POLICY-1604.  alpine openjdk8 deploys with a smaller set of libraries than previous version.   The one that is causing these problems are libnss3.so in particular.   It seems that the openjdk8 version was released 2 days ago, which explains that we are seeing these problems now.

Pam, I replied you on the jira board.
